titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the André Bertrand and André Schneider Papers
titlestmt.author: by Nora Soto

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday December 10, 2024 at 23:06

did.unittitle: André Bertrand and André Schneider papers

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: In French, Dutch, English, and Spanish.

did.abstract: The collection contains correspondence, organizational files, communiques, political tracts, pamphlets, comics, posters, press clippings, books, and computer media by or relating to André Bertrand, André Schneider, and the 1966 student protest movement at the Université de Strasbourg in France.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p:
This collection is open for research.
Box 9 (electronic media): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from André Bertrand and André Schneider on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2018.
Purchased from Didier Lecointre on the William A. Taylor Memorial Fund, 2024.
Purchased from Didier Lecointre-Dominique Drouet on the William A. Taylor Memorial Fund, 2024, 2024.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into eighteen series: I. Correspondence, 1965–2013. II. Papers Relating to Strasbourg Student Protests, 2010–2016. III. Association Fédérative Générale des Étudiants de Strasbourg (AFGES) Papers, 1963–2017. IV. Materials Relating to Les Garnautins, 1967–2010s. V. Renseignements Généraux Reports, 2014. VI. Press, 1966–2014. VII. Strasbourg Trials, 1967–2010s. VIII. Daniel Joubert Papers, 1960–2017. IX. Papers Relating to May '68 Protests, 1967–2012. X. Comics and Posters, 1968–2017. XI. Seventh Conference of the International Situationniste, 1966. XII. English and Spanish Publications, 1965–1973. XIII. Provo Material, 1966. XIV. Printed Material, 1965–1997. XV. Computer Media, circa 2014. April 2024 acquisition [1 of 3], 1964. April 2024 acquisition [2 of 3], 1966. April 2024 acquisition [3 of 3], 1967.

bioghist.head: André Bertrand (1945-) and André Schneider (1943-)
bioghist.p: André Bertrand and André Schneider were French students at the Université de Strasbourg and major participants in the student protest movement in the fall of 1966. Both men were influenced by the Internationale situationniste, an anti-capitalist revolutionary movement in France and across Europe, as well as by anarchism and the Dutch Provo movement. In May 1966, Schneider was elected president of the Association fédérative générale des étudiants de Strasbourg (AFGES), the university's chapter of the French national student union, alongside other pro-Situationist student officers. Under Schneider's leadership, the AFGES began to collaborate with Guy Debord, a principal member of the Internationale situationniste, and situationist activist Mustapha Khayati over the summer. The small group of AFGES officers, university students, and Situationists launched a series of protests in October 1966, which included disrupting classes and distributing Bertrand's Situationist-inspired comic Le retour de la colonne Durutti across campus. In November 1966, the movement culminated in what became known as the Strasbourg scandal, wherein the AFGES illegally used student union funds to print 10,000 copies of De la misère en milieu étudiants ( On the Poverty of Student Life ), a pro-Situationist pamphlet written primarily by Khayati. The public outcry resulting from the Strasbourg scandal shut down the campus and led to the dissolution of the AFGES by court order. A precursor to the general strike of May 1968, the Strasbourg student protest movement received extensive press coverage and contributed to the wider dissemination of Situationist ideas.

Bertrand continued work as an artist in the style of détournement, and Schneider later became a professor of history and geography. In 2018, they published Le scandale de Strasbourg mis à nu par ses célibataires, même , which documented the 1966 protests.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: André Bertrand and André Schneider Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing. Information in brackets is also supplied by staff.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection contains correspondence, organizational files, communiques, political tracts, pamphlets, comics and works of détournement, posters, press clippings, books, and computer media by or relating to André Bertrand, André Schneider, and the 1966 student protest movement at the Université de Strasbourg in France. Correspondence documents collaboration between members of the Internationale situationniste, Strasbourg students, and other activists, such as Guy Debord, Raoul Hausmann, and Sean Wilder. Papers concern the events of the Strasbourg scandal, including the actions of the student union Association fédérative générale des étudiants de Strasbourg (AFGES), the expulsion of the student group known as the Garnautins from Internationale situationniste, and court proceedings. There are also papers pertaining to Daniel Joubert, another major figure in the protests. Other materials document the May 1968 general strike in Strasbourg and various French cities, left-wing activism in the United States, and the Provo movement in the Netherlands. Also present are works by American writer and translator Ken Knabb, French philosopher Jean-Pierre Voyer, and other authors.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The André Bertrand and André Schneider Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

corpname.part: Association fédérative générale des étudiants de Strasbourg

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of the correspondence of André Bertrand and André Schneider, as well as correspondence between members of the Internationale situationniste and other students at the University of Strasbourg. Included are Bertrand's letters to Sean Wilder, an American student involved with the French Situationists; Schneider's exchanges with Austrian Dadaist artist Raoul Hausmann, including carbon copies of Hausmann's Dada texts; and photocopies of letters by Guy Debord, a French founding member of Internationale situationniste.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: André Bertrand and André Schneider Correspondence and Third Party Correspondence. Each subseries is arranged alphabetically by correspondent.

did.unittitle: André Bertrand and André Schneider Correspondence
Schneider, André to and from Raoul Hausmann
Debord, Guy and Internationale situationniste members to Sean Wilder [photocopies]
Papers Relating to Strasbourg Student Protests

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains autobiographical writings and testimonies by other participants in the 1966 protests at the University of Strasbourg, as well as photographs of student activists and Situationists collected for the book Le scandale de Strasbourg mis à nu par ses célibataires, même . Included are accounts by Sean Wilder and Bruno Vayr-Piova, vice president of the AFGES during the protests.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

did.unittitle: Association Fédérative Générale des Étudiants de Strasbourg (AFGES) Papers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of manuscripts, tracts, pamphlets, and press clippings relating to the AFGES student union at the University of Strasbourg and its involvement in the 1966 protests under the pro-Situationist leadership of Schneider and Bruno Vayr-Piova. Also present is material commemorating the fiftieth anniversary of the protests.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

did.unittitle: Ensemble de documents publiés à l'occasion de la commémoration du scandale à Strasbourg

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains tracts, posters, and communiques relating to the Garnautins, a group of Strasbourg students who were members of the Internationale situationniste until their expulsion from the organization in January 1967. Three students — Theo Frey, Jean Garnault, and Herbert Holl — were officially excluded by the Situationists, while a fourth, Edith Frey, resigned alongside them. The Garnautins had participated in the 1966 protests and were involved with the Strasbourg chapter of the Mutuelle nationale des étudiants de France (MNEF).

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

did.unittitle: Mutuelle nationale des étudiants de France (MNEF)
Mutuelle nationale des étudiants de France (MNEF)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains Schneider's photocopies of reports on the 1966 Strasbourg protests by the French intelligence service Renseignements généraux.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains press clippings of local, regional, and national French newspapers documenting the Strasbourg protests and its aftermath.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains legal documents and press clippings relating to court trials arising from the Strasbourg protests.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

did.unittitle: La rapport Wagner, huissier de justice
Ensemble de photocopies de coupures de presse relatifs au "scandale de Strasbourg"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains writings, clippings, and printed material by or relating to Daniel Joubert (1939–1996), a French writer who was a major participant in the 1966 Strasbourg protests. He met Bertrand and Schneider in 1963 while studying at the University of Strasbourg and was influential in disseminating Situationist ideas to the group. In the early 1960s, Joubert was editor of Notes critiques , the journal of Bordeaux-based autonomist group Arguments, and contributed articles to the Protestant student journal Le Semeur from 1962 to 1965. During the May 1968 general strike, he was active in the Conseil pour le maintien des occupations (CMDO).

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

did.unittitle: "Association hétérogène ou communisme?" [with "Correspondance avec un philosophe"]
"Un exemple de l'influence de Hegel en France au dix-neuvième siècle: Mallarmé"

title.part: Notes critiques: Bulletin de recherché et d'orientation révolutionnaire

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains tracts, pamphlets, posters, and comics relating to the general strike of May 1968 at the universities of Paris, Nantes, Toulouse, and Strasbourg. Included is material produced by the CMDO and Comité révolutionnaire de l'université autonome de Strasbourg.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

did.unittitle: Tracts publiés par le conseil de Nantes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains original copies and reproductions of comic strips and posters by Bertrand and other activists. Included are works of détournement by the Comité de réhabilitation de Karl Marx et Ravachol, a Situationist-inspired anarchist group founded at the University of Strasbourg; three issues of Gertrude , a comic magazine featuring contributions by Bertrand, Schneider, and Daniel Joubert; and Bertrand's more recent design work from 2014 to 2016.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

did.unittitle: Comics et projets d'affiches de l'Atelier graphique du Comité de réhabilitation de Karl Marx et Ravachol
Comics et projets d'affiches de l'Atelier graphique du Comité de réhabilitation de Karl Marx et Ravachol
Seventh Conference of the International Situationniste

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains reports and handbills distributed at the seventh Situationist conference held in Paris July 5–13, 1966. The conference was attended by Strasbourg students Edith Frey, Theo Frey, Jean Garnault, and Herbert Holl.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains English- and Spanish-language tracts, pamphlets, and comics produced by left-wing activists and organizations in the United States. It includes works by Ken Knabb and translations of French Situationist tracts.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

did.unittitle: "El pasado se desnace ; Los juegos se hacen"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains Dutch-language tracts, pamphlets, and posters relating to Provo, a counterculture movement active in the Netherlands from 1965 to 1967.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains 19 French-language books, pamphlets, and brochures collected by Bertrand and Schneider. Included are Situationist, post-Situationist, and anarchist publications by Guy Debord, Daniel Joubert, Ken Knabb, Jean-Pierre Voyer, and other writers.

did.unittitle: Arno, Toni and J. P. Dauvergne. L'obscurité du moment non-vécu
Barrot, Jean. Violence et solidarité révolutionnaires: Les procès des communistes de Barcelone
Beaufils, Pierre and Philippe Locuratolo. Apologie de Jacques Bonhomme: Critique de la politique économique
Béhouir, Bartholomé. De la conciergerie internationale des situationnistes
Debord, Guy. Correspondance avec un cybernéticien
Internationale situationniste. Le point d'explosion de l'idéologie en Chine
Modzelewski, Karol and Jacek Kuroń. Lettre ouverte au parti ouvrier polonais
Joubert, Daniel. Marx versus Stirner ; Le fantôme de l'opéré
Knabb, Ken. La réalisation et la suppression de la religion
Knabb, Ken. Remarques sur le groupe Contradiction et son échec
Voyer, Jean-Pierre. A un pédé mondain
Voyer, Jean-Pierre. Introduction à la science de la publicité
Voyer, Jean-Pierre. Limites de conversation II
Voyer, Jean-Pierre. Limites de conversation III
Voyer, Jean-Pierre. Rapport sur l'état des illusions dans notre parti suivi de révélations sur le principe du monde

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains three compact discs.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 9 (electronic media): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
